# ML Toolbox — Vision

<!-- USER_SECTION_START -->
## Goals
- implement phase 1：
---
# ml-toolbox — Architecture Plan

> **Personal project.** Single-user, runs on a Mac Mini (local network only).
> No auth, no multi-tenancy, no SLA. Build it right, not enterprise-ready.

---

## Overview

This phase builds the complete foundation: node protocol, canvas UI, execution engine,
and sandbox infrastructure. No ML nodes yet — those come next, once the platform is solid.

Done correctly, any node following the `@node` protocol can be dropped into the canvas
and run safely in an isolated Docker container. The next phase is purely about writing
those nodes — no infrastructure changes required.

---

## Stack

```
Frontend:
  React 19
  TypeScript 5
  Vite 6
  Tailwind CSS v4           (@tailwindcss/vite plugin — no postcss needed)
  shadcn/ui                 (fully supports React 19 + Tailwind v4)
  @xyflow/react 12.10.1     (React Flow — package renamed from reactflow)
  @monaco-editor/react      (Python syntax highlighting + IntelliSense)
  pnpm

Backend:
  Python 3.13
  FastAPI 0.135.1           (fastapi[standard])
  uv
  Polars 1.39.0             (data processing between nodes)
  pandas 2.x                (final step — sklearn compatibility bridge)
  pyarrow 23.0.1            (Arrow/Parquet file format for node output storage)
  pydantic v2               (data validation, bundled with FastAPI)
  docker-py                 (Python Docker SDK — spawn sandbox containers)

Deployment:
  Docker Compose (Mac Mini — minyis-mac-mini, linux/arm64)
  Caddy 2                   (reverse proxy + static file server — frontend/API on one port)
  nginx                     (already configured: /ml-toolbox/ → localhost:8910)
  GHA: CI on PR (ruff format + pyright + pytest + eslint + tsc)
       CD on push to main (docker build linux/arm64 + SSH deploy via Tailscale)
  Port: 8910
  Vite base: "/ml-toolbox/"  (required so assets resolve correctly under subpath)
  GitHub repo: https://github.com/minyic7/ml-toolbox
  GHCR images:
    ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/backend:latest
    ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/sandbox:latest

  Mac Mini setup (already done):
    ~/ml-toolbox/          ← repo cloned here
    ~/ml-toolbox/frontend/dist/  ← created, rsync target
    nginx /ml-toolbox/ → localhost:8910  ← configured and restarted
    ~/.ssh/ml_toolbox_deploy  ← deploy key generated + added to authorized_keys

  GitHub Secrets (all set):
    TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID   ← Tailscale OAuth
    TS_OAUTH_SECRET      ← Tailscale OAuth
    DEPLOY_SSH_KEY       ← ed25519 deploy key
    DEPLOY_USER          ← minyic
    DEPLOY_HOST          ← minyis-mac-mini

  Upstream nginx (already active):
    location = /ml-toolbox {
        return 301 /ml-toolbox/;
    }
    location /ml-toolbox/ {
        proxy_pass http://host.docker.internal:8910/;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
  Note: trailing slash on proxy_pass strips the /ml-toolbox/ prefix — Caddy receives
  requests starting with / and needs no changes.
```

---

## Core Design Decisions

### Data Transfer Between Nodes

Node outputs are written to **structured files on disk**. Nodes receive **file paths**, not data objects.

```
Data directory (default: ~/.ml-toolbox, override via ML_TOOLBOX_DATA_DIR env var):

~/.ml-toolbox/
  projects/
    {project_id}/
      pipeline.json              ← saved pipeline state
      runs/
        {run_id}/
          {node_id}.parquet      ← TABLE  (Polars DataFrame)
          {node_id}.pkl          ← MODEL  (any model object)
          {node_id}.json         ← METRICS / VALUE
          {node_id}.npy          ← ARRAY  (np.ndarray)
          {node_id}.pt           ← TENSOR (torch.Tensor)
          {node_id}_manifest.json         ← execution input manifest
          {node_id}_manifest_result.json  ← execution output manifest
          {node_id}.hash                  ← params + code SHA-256
```

Benefits:
- No memory limit — large datasets handled transparently
- Natural isolation — nodes cannot mutate upstream data
- Per-project isolation — different pipelines never share files
- Run history preserved — previous execution outputs remain accessible
- Debug-friendly — files persist for inspection when Keep Outputs is ON
- Docker volume mount on this directory → data survives container restarts
- Sandbox containers mount the same run directory — no extra file transfer needed

**Pipeline Settings: Keep Intermediate Outputs** (default: ON)
- ON  → run directory kept after execution; user can download any node output from Output tab
- OFF → run directory cleaned up after pipeline completes successfully

If pipeline errors mid-run, all outputs up to the failed node are preserved regardless of this setting.

The data directory is configured in `config.py`:
```python
DATA_DIR = Path(os.environ.get("ML_TOOLBOX_DATA_DIR", Path.home() / ".ml-toolbox"))
```

### Pipeline Storage

Pipeline state is stored as **`pipeline.json` directly in each project directory** — no database required.

```python
# ml_toolbox/services/store.py

def save(pipeline_id: str, data: dict) -> None:
    path = DATA_DIR / "projects" / pipeline_id / "pipeline.json"
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2))

def load(pipeline_id: str) -> dict:
    path = DATA_DIR / "projects" / pipeline_id / "pipeline.json"
    return json.loads(path.read_text())

def list_all() -> list[dict]:
    projects = DATA_DIR / "projects"
    if not projects.exists():
        return []
    dirs = sorted(projects.iterdir(), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
    return [load(p.name) for p in dirs if (p / "pipeline.json").exists()]

def delete(pipeline_id: str) -> None:
    path = DATA_DIR / "projects" / pipeline_id
    shutil.rmtree(path, ignore_errors=True)
```

Benefits:
- No extra dependencies
- Consistent with the existing directory structure — pipeline state lives alongside its run outputs
- Human-readable: `cat pipeline.json` for debugging
- No locking issues

### Pipeline Execution

DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) — directed, no cycles allowed.

```
Execution rules:
- Run All   → always creates a new run_id, starts from all root nodes
- Run From  → always creates a new run_id, re-runs node + all downstream;
              upstream cached outputs are hard-linked into the new run dir
              (so every run directory is fully self-contained and historically traceable)
- Skip a node only if: output file exists in current run dir AND params hash matches
  (params change → node is dirty → must re-run)
- Cycle detection at connect time → reject connection if it would form a cycle
```

**Run ID lifecycle**: every execution creates a fresh `{run_id}/` directory (UUID).
`Run From` copies upstream cached results into the new dir via hard links
(same inode, zero disk cost on same filesystem) before starting execution.
This means each run dir is a complete snapshot — no ambiguity about which files
belong to which run.

**Params hash**: each node stores a SHA-256 of its serialised params + code alongside its
output file. On skip check: recompute hash, compare with stored. Mismatch → re-run.

context: .
          file: Dockerfile
          push: true
          platforms: linux/arm64           # Mac Mini M4
          tags: ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/backend:latest
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  build-sandbox:
    name: Build sandbox
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.sandbox == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: ./backend/sandbox
          push: true
          platforms: linux/arm64           # Mac Mini M4
          tags: ghcr.io/minyic7/ml-toolbox/sandbox:latest
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max
          # numpy/pyarrow/polars layer almost always cache hit
          # only runner.py changes rebuild the last layer (~5s)

  build-frontend:
    name: Build frontend
    needs: detect-changes
    if: needs.detect-changes.outputs.frontend == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
        with:
          version: 9
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/pnpm-lock.yaml
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
        working-directory: frontend
      - run: pnpm build
        working-directory: frontend
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: frontend-dist
          path: frontend/dist/
          retention-days: 1

  deploy:
    name: Deploy
    needs: [build-backend, build-sandbox, build-frontend]
    if: |
      always() &&
      !contains(needs.*.result, 'failure') &&
      !contains(needs.*.result, 'cancelled') &&
      (
        needs.build-backend.result  == 'success' ||
        needs.build-sandbox.result  == 'success' ||
        needs.build-frontend.result == 'success'
      )
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Connect Tailscale
        uses: tailscale/github-action@v2
        with:
          oauth-client-id: ${{ secrets.TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID }}
          oauth-secret:    ${{ secrets.TS_OAUTH_SECRET }}
          tags: tag:ci

      - name: Setup SSH
        run: |
          echo "${{ secrets.DEPLOY_SSH_KEY }}" > /tmp/deploy_key
          chmod 600 /tmp/deploy_key

      - name: Upload compose files
        run: |
          scp -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            docker-compose.yml Caddyfile \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:~/ml-toolbox/

      - name: Sync frontend
        if: needs.build-frontend.result == 'success'
        run: |
          rsync -az --delete -e "ssh -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no" \
            frontend/dist/ \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}:~/ml-toolbox/frontend/dist/

      - name: Deploy containers
        if: |
          needs.build-backend.result == 'success' ||
          needs.build-sandbox.result == 'success'
        run: |
          ssh -i /tmp/deploy_key -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no \
            ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}@${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} << 'EOF'
            cd ~/ml-toolbox
            echo ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} | docker login ghcr.io -u ${{ github.actor }} --password-stdin
            docker compose pull
            docker compose up -d --no-build
            docker image prune -f
          EOF
```

### Secrets

```
TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID   ← Tailscale OAuth client ID  (set ✓)
TS_OAUTH_SECRET      ← Tailscale OAuth secret      (set ✓)
DEPLOY_SSH_KEY       ← ed25519 deploy key          (set ✓)
DEPLOY_USER          ← minyic                      (set ✓)
DEPLOY_HOST          ← minyis-mac-mini             (set ✓)
GITHUB_TOKEN         ← auto-provided by GHA, no setup needed
```

### Estimated times per scenario

| Scenario | CI | CD |
|---|---|---|
| Frontend only | both jobs ~75s | pnpm build + rsync ~15s (no container restart) |
| Backend logic only | both jobs ~75s | build-backend arm64 (cache hit) ~60s + deploy ~15s |
| runner.py only | both jobs ~75s | build-sandbox (last layer only) ~15s + deploy ~15s |
| Sandbox deps changed | both jobs ~75s | build-sandbox (Layer 2+) ~3-4min |
| Everything changed | both jobs parallel ~75s | three builds parallel + deploy |

### docker-compose.yml on Mac Mini

```yaml
services:
  caddy:
    image: caddy:2-alpine
    ports:
      - "8910:80"
    volumes:
      - ./Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile:ro
      - ./frontend/dist:/srv/frontend:ro   # bind mount — rsync writes here directly
    depends_on:
      - backend

  backend:
    image: ghcr.io/{github_username}/ml-toolbox/backend:latest
    volumes:
      - ml_data:/root/.ml-toolbox
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock

  sandbox:
    image: ghcr.io/{github_username}/ml-toolbox/sandbox:latest
    profiles: ["build-only"]

volumes:
  ml_data:
```

`docker-compose.dev.yml` keeps `build:` directives for local development.

---

## What Comes Next

Once this foundation is running, the only work left is **writing nodes**. No infrastructure
changes needed — just new files under `nodes/` following the `@node` protocol:

```
nodes/
  ingest/
    file_input.py       # CSV / Parquet / Excel → TABLE
    sql_input.py        # SQL query → TABLE
  transform/
    clean.py            # null handling, type casting
    feature_eng.py      # scaling, encoding, binning
    split.py            # train/test split → TABLE, TABLE
  train/
    sklearn_train.py    # fit any sklearn estimator → MODEL
    xgb_train.py
  evaluate/
    classification.py   # accuracy, F1, ROC → METRICS
    regression.py       # RMSE, MAE, R² → METRICS
  export/
    export_table.py     # TABLE → CSV / Parquet download
    export_model.py     # MODEL → .pkl download
```

Each node is a self-contained Python file. The sandbox handles isolation automatically.
---

WebSocket WS /ws/pipelines/{id} → real-time execution status per node ``` ### Pipeline JSON Schema ```json { "id": "uuid", "name": "My Pipeline", "settings": { "keep_outputs": true }, "nodes": [ { "id": "instance_uuid", "type": "clean", "position": {"x": 100, "y": 200}, "params": {"strategy": "mean"}, "code": "def run(inputs, params):\n ..." } ], "edges": [ { "id": "edge_uuid", "source": "instance_uuid_a", "source_port": "df", "target": "instance_uuid_b", "target_port": "df", "condition": null } ] } ``` ### WebSocket Message Format ```json { "node_id": "instance_uuid", "status": "running | done | error | skipped", "outputs": {"df": "/tmp/..."}, "traceback": "..." } ``` --- ## 3. Execution Engine See full implementation in Core Design Decisions → Node Execution Sandbox above. ### Cycle Detection At edge creation time (frontend + backend validation): ```python def would_create_cycle(pipeline, source_id, target_id) -> bool: # DFS from target — if we can reach source, adding this edge creates a cycle visited = set() stack = [target_id] while stack: node = stack.pop() if node == source_id: return True if node not in visited: visited.add(node) stack.extend(pipeline.get_downstream(node)) return False ``` --- ## 4. content.content:
**Sandbox image** — built once at deploy time, reused for every node execution.
Deps are only downloaded during `docker build`, never at runtime:

```dockerfile
# backend/sandbox/Dockerfile

FROM python:3.13-slim

# Layer 1: heavy packages that rarely change (best cache hit rate)
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir numpy pyarrow polars

# Layer 2: packages that change occasionally
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir pandas scikit-learn

# Layer 3: runner script (changes most often — last for cache efficiency)
WORKDIR /sandbox
COPY runner.py /sandbox/runner.py
```

Changing `runner.py` only invalidates the last layer.
Adding a new ML package only invalidates Layer 2 forward.
The heavy numpy/pyarrow/polars layer is almost never re-downloaded.

```python
# backend/sandbox/runner.py — runs inside the container

import json, sys
from pathlib import Path

manifest_path = Path(sys.argv[1])
manifest      = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text())

code    = manifest["code"]
inputs  = manifest["inputs"]   # already /data/... paths inside the container
params  = manifest["params"]
node_id = manifest["node_id"]
run_dir = manifest_path.parent

def _get_output_path(name: str = "output", ext: str = ".parquet") -> str:
    return str(run_dir / f"{node_id}_{name}{ext}")

# Inject _get_output_path so node code can call it without any imports
namespace = {"_get_output_path": _get_output_path}
exec(code, namespace)
result = namespace["run"](inputs, params)

out_path = manifest_path.parent / (manifest_path.stem + "_result.json")
out_path.write_text(json.dumps(result))
```

content.content:
### Graph Structure

- One or more root nodes (no inputs, e.g. FileInput)
- One or more leaf nodes (no outputs, e.g. ExportTable)
- Branching and merging allowed
- No cycles — enforced at edge creation time

---

## 1. Node Protocol

Every node — built-in or user-defined — uses the `@node` decorator.

```python
# ml_toolbox/protocol/decorators.py

@node(
    inputs={"df": TABLE},
    outputs={"df": TABLE},
    params={
        "strategy": Select(["mean", "median", "mode", "drop"], default="mean")
    }
)
def run(inputs: dict, params: dict) -> dict:
    """
    inputs:  {"df": "/data/node_abc.parquet"}   ← container-internal path
    params:  {"strategy": "mean"}
    returns: {"df": "/data/node_xyz.parquet"}
    """
    import polars as pl
    df = pl.read_parquet(inputs["df"])
    strategy = params["strategy"]
    if strategy == "drop":
        df = df.drop_nulls()
    else:
        df = df.fill_null(strategy=strategy)
    output_path = _get_output_path()
    df.write_parquet(output_path)
    return {"df": output_path}
```

### Port Types

```python
# ml_toolbox/protocol/types.py

class PortType(str, Enum):
    TABLE   = "TABLE"    # Polars DataFrame (stored as Parquet)
    MODEL   = "MODEL"    # any model object — sklearn, xgb, lgbm, torch (stored as pickle)
    METRICS = "METRICS"  # dict of evaluation results (stored as JSON)
    ARRAY   = "ARRAY"    # np.ndarray (stored as .npy)
    VALUE   = "VALUE"    # scalar: int / float / str / bool (stored as JSON)
    TENSOR  = "TENSOR"   # torch.Tensor / tf.Tensor (stored as .pt or .npy)

# Frontend port colors
PORT_COLORS = {
    "TABLE":   "#9CA3AF",  # grey
    "MODEL":   "#22C55E",  # green
    "METRICS": "#EAB308",  # yellow
    "ARRAY":   "#3B82F6",  # blue
    "VALUE":   "#A855F7",  # purple
    "TENSOR":  "#F97316",  # orange
}
```

Adding a new type = one line in the enum + one line in PORT_COLORS. No other changes required.

## Repository Exploration Complete

I have thoroughly explored the ML Toolbox repository and gathered comprehensive information about the project architecture, API schema, and file structure. Here's what I found:

### Project Overview

**ML Toolbox** is a visual node-based ML workflow platform for single users running on a Mac Mini (local network only). The architecture separates the platform into three layers:

1. **Frontend** - Visual canvas with node-based UI
2. **Backend** - REST API + WebSocket server  
3. **Execution Sandbox** - Isolated Docker containers for node execution

### Key Files and Locations

**Vision Document:**
- `/data/clones/b754ab4b-2c5` on the `remotes/origin/kanban-claude-hub` branch contains the complete VISION.md file

**Current Project State:**
- Main branch: minimal README only
- Feature branches exist but are incomplete:
  - `remotes/origin/feature/scaffold-backend-fastapi-uv-docker-3f0f47` (backend skeleton)
  - `remotes/origin/feature/scaffold-frontend-react-19-vite-6-tailwi-b754ab` (frontend skeleton)
- Currently on: `feature/scaffold-frontend-react-19-vite-6-tailwi-b754ab` branch

### Stack & Technology

**Frontend:**
- React 19, TypeScript 5, Vite 6
- Tailwind CSS v4 (@tailwindcss/vite plugin)
- shadcn/ui, @xyflow/react 12.10.1, @monaco-editor/react
- pnpm package manager
- Base path: "/ml-toolbox/"

**Backend:**
- Python 3.13, FastAPI 0.135.1
- uv package manager
- Polars 1.39.0, pandas 2.x, pyarrow 23.0.1, pydantic v2, docker-py

**Deployment:**
- Docker Compose (arm64 for Mac Mini M4)
- Caddy 2 reverse proxy
- Port: 8910
- GitHub Actions CI/CD

### API Endpoints Schema

**Node Library:**
- GET `/api/nodes` - list all registered node definitions
- GET `/api/nodes/{node_type}` - single node definition + default code

**Pipeline CRUD:**
- POST/GET/PUT/DELETE `/api/pipelines`

- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/duplicate`

**Node Operations:**
- POST/DELETE `/api/pipelines/{id}/nodes`
- PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/nodes/{node_id}`

**Edge Operations:**
- POST/DELETE/PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/edges`

**Pipeline Settings & Execution:**
- PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/settings`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/run`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/run/{node_id}`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/cancel`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/status`

**Run History & Outputs:**
- GET/DELETE `/api/pipelines/{id}/runs`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}?run_id=`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}/download?run_id=`

**WebSocket:**
- WS `/ws/pipelines/{id}` - real-time execution status

### Data Models

**Pipeline JSON Schema:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "name": "string",
  "settings": { "keep_outputs": boolean },
  "nodes": [{"id": "uuid", "type": "string", "position": {x, y}, "params": {}, "code": "string"}],
  "edges": [{"id": "uuid", "source": "uuid", "source_port": "string", "target": "uuid", "target_port": "string", "condition": null}]
}
```

**Node Protocol - Port Types:**
- TABLE (Parquet) - grey #9CA3AF
- MODEL (pickle) - green #22C55E
- METRICS (JSON) - yellow #EAB308
- ARRAY (npy) - blue #3B82F6
- VALUE (JSON) - purple #A855F7
- TENSOR (pt/npy) - orange #F97316

**Node Protocol - Param Types:**
- Select(options, default)
- Slider(min, max, step, default)
- Text(default)
- Toggle(default)

**WebSocket Message Format:**
```json
{
  "node_id": "uuid",
  "status": "running|done|error|skipped",
  "outputs": {"port_name": "/path/..."},
  "traceback": "..."
}
```

### Project Directory Structure

  backend/
    src/ml_toolbox/
      main.py, config.py

## Repository Exploration Complete

I have thoroughly explored the ML Toolbox repository and gathered comprehensive information about the project architecture, API schema, and file structure. Here's what I found:

### Project Overview

**ML Toolbox** is a visual node-based ML workflow platform for single users running on a Mac Mini (local network only). The architecture separates the platform into three layers:

1. **Frontend** - Visual canvas with node-based UI
2. **Backend** - REST API + WebSocket server  
3. **Execution Sandbox** - Isolated Docker containers for node execution

### Key Files and Locations

**Vision Document:**
- `/data/clones/b754ab4b-2c5` on the `remotes/origin/kanban-claude-hub` branch contains the complete VISION.md file

**Current Project State:**
- Main branch: minimal README only
- Feature branches exist but are incomplete:
  - `remotes/origin/feature/scaffold-backend-fastapi-uv-docker-3f0f47` (backend skeleton)
  - `remotes/origin/feature/scaffold-frontend-react-19-vite-6-tailwi-b754ab` (frontend skeleton)
- Currently on: `feature/scaffold-frontend-react-19-vite-6-tailwi-b754ab` branch

### Stack & Technology

**Frontend:**
- React 19, TypeScript 5, Vite 6
- Tailwind CSS v4 (@tailwindcss/vite plugin)
- shadcn/ui, @xyflow/react 12.10.1, @monaco-editor/react
- pnpm package manager
- Base path: "/ml-toolbox/"

**Backend:**
- Python 3.13, FastAPI 0.135.1
- uv package manager
- Polars 1.39.0, pandas 2.x, pyarrow 23.0.1, pydantic v2, docker-py

**Deployment:**
- Docker Compose (arm64 for Mac Mini M4)
- Caddy 2 reverse proxy
- Port: 8910
- GitHub Actions CI/CD

### API Endpoints Schema

**Node Library:**
- GET `/api/nodes` - list all registered node definitions
- GET `/api/nodes/{node_type}` - single node definition + default code

**Pipeline CRUD:**
- POST/GET/PUT/DELETE `/api/pipelines`
- GET/DELETE `/api/pipelines/{id}`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/duplicate`

**Node Operations:**
- POST/DELETE `/api/pipelines/{id}/nodes`
- PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/nodes/{node_id}`

**Edge Operations:**
- POST/DELETE/PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/edges`

**Pipeline Settings & Execution:**
- PATCH `/api/pipelines/{id}/settings`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/run`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/run/{node_id}`
- POST `/api/pipelines/{id}/cancel`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/status`

**Run History & Outputs:**
- GET/DELETE `/api/pipelines/{id}/runs`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}?run_id=`
- GET `/api/pipelines/{id}/outputs/{node_id}/download?run_id=`

**WebSocket:**
- WS `/ws/pipelines/{id}` - real-time execution status

### Data Models

**Pipeline JSON Schema:**
```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "name": "string",
  "settings": { "keep_outputs": boolean },
  "nodes": [{"id": "uuid", "type": "string", "position": {x, y}, "params": {}, "code": "string"}],
  "edges": [{"id": "uuid", "source": "uuid", "source_port": "string", "target": "uuid", "target_port": "string", "condition": null}]
}
```

**Node Protocol - Port Types:**
- TABLE (Parquet) - grey #9CA3AF
- MODEL (pickle) - green #22C55E
- METRICS (JSON) - yellow #EAB308
- ARRAY (npy) - blue #3B82F6
- VALUE (JSON) - purple #A855F7
- TENSOR (pt/npy) - orange #F97316

**Node Protocol - Param Types:**
- Select(options, default)
- Slider(min, max, step, default)
- Text(default)
- Toggle(default)

**WebSocket Message Format:**
```json
{
  "node_id": "uuid",
  "status": "running|done|error|skipped",
  "outputs": {"port_name": "/path/..."},
  "traceback": "..."
}
```

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Scaffold frontend with React 19, Vite 6, Tailwind v4, and shadcn/ui

Set up complete frontend skeleton:
- React 19 + TypeScript 5 + Vite 6 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin
- shadcn/ui initialized with Button component
- 3-panel layout (sidebar, canvas, right panel) with placeholder components
- TypeScript interfaces for all API types (Pipeline, Node, Edge, Port, etc.)
- API client with all endpoint functions typed and stubbed
- Path aliases (@/ → src/), strict TypeScript, base path /ml-toolbox/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: Those two commits have a huge diff because of node_modules. Let me squash them into a single clean commit to avoid pushing a massive history.

input.command:
git reset --soft HEAD~2 && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Scaffold frontend with React 19, Vite 6, Tailwind v4, and shadcn/ui

Set up complete frontend skeleton:
- React 19 + TypeScript 5 + Vite 6 with @tailwindcss/vite plugin
- shadcn/ui initialized with Button component
- 3-panel layout (sidebar, canvas, right panel) with placeholder components
- TypeScript interfaces for all API types (Pipeline, Node, Edge, Port, etc.)
- API client with all endpoint functions typed and stubbed
- Path aliases (@/ → src/), strict TypeScript, base path /ml-toolbox/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Squash into clean commit without node_modules
